perturbation.original_text: References to clauses and parties are references to clauses of, and parties t o, this agreement; references to things include parts of those things; references to persons include legal and natural persons; and words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa.
perturbation.changed_text: References to clauses and parties are references to clauses of, and parties t o, this agreement; references to things include parts of those things; references to persons include legal and natural persons.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa', the agreement's interpretation becomes ambiguous regarding singular/plural terms. This creates a contradiction if, for instance, 'party' is used elsewhere, and it's unclear if it refers to one or multiple parties, especially if another section references the singular/plural interpretation. If 'party' or 'parties' is referenced elsewhere in the text, and this definition has now been altered, this will create an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties are independent parties and nothing in this agreement constitutes a joint venture, partnership or agency. Neither party has, nor may hold itself out as having, any obligation to perform any activity or to enter into any business relationship in relation to the purpose for which confidential information was made available. Neither party may make any public announcement or press release c oncerning the purpose or this agreement without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that singular terms include the plural and vice versa. The changed text removes this definition. The contradicted text uses the term 'parties'. Without the definition provided by the original text, it becomes unclear whether 'parties' refers to a single party or multiple parties, creating ambiguity and a contradiction. This ambiguity impacts the interpretation and enforceability of the clause concerning independent entities and their obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Confidentiality & Non -Disclosure Agreement ICT Strategic Consulting 1 of 2 Ph: +61 (02) 9591 1464 56 Minnamorra Avenue Earlwood NSW 2206 Australia www.ICTStrategicConsulting.com 1. PARTIES AND DATE: This agreement is made on: [Date] between: (1) [Company] of [Address] ; and (2) ICT Strategic Consulting , 56 Minnamorra Avenue, Earlwood NSW, 2206 Australia (“ICTS C”).
perturbation.changed_text: Confidentiality & Non -Disclosure Agreement 1 of 2 1. PARTIES AND DATE: This agreement is made on: [Date] between: (1) [Company] of [Address] ; and (2) ICT Strategic Consulting .
perturbation.explanation: Removing the contact information creates an issue if the agreement is breached and legal notices need to be served. This alteration contradicts the implicit assumption that the parties can be contacted through reasonable means, if there isn't another section containing this information. It contradicts the ability to enforce the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of each party disclosing its Confidential Information to the other party for the Purpose, the Recipient must ensure that Confidential Information is:  maintained confidential with at least the standard of care that the Recipient maintains t o protect its own confidential information;  not disclosed to any Unauthorised Person;  only used for the Purpose; and  not reproduced in any form except as required for the purpose. The Recipient must ensure that access to Confidential Information is only given to those of its officers, employees and advisers who require access for the Purpose, and that those officers, employees and advisers are informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information an d kee p that information confidential. Parties to whom the information is imparted must be warned that include d ‘insider information ,’ regarding publicly listed and traded shares may preclud e them from such trade in those shares . The Confidential Informatio n remains at all times the property of the Discloser, and other than as provided in this agreement, no rights in respect of the Confidential Information are granted or conveyed to the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes the contact information of ICT Strategic Consulting. The changed text removes this contact information. The explanation states that removing the contact information creates an issue if the agreement is breached and legal notices need to be served, thus contradicting the ability to enforce the agreement. The contradicted text outlines the responsibilities of the recipient of confidential information, and implicitly assumes parties can be contacted. Therefore, the change creates a direct contradiction by removing means of contact, hindering enforcement of the recipient's responsibilities regarding confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Recipient is legally required to disclo se any Confidential Information, the Recipient must immediately notify the Discloser of that fact. Within 7 days of a written request by the Discloser, the Recipient must return all Confidential Information, and provide a signed certificate stating that al l Confidential Information in the Recipient's possession or control has been returned.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Recipient is legally required to disclose any Confidential Information, the Recipient must immediately notify the Discloser of that fact.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Within 7 days of a written request by the Discloser, the Recipient must return all Confidential Information, and provide a signed certificate stating that al l Confidential Information in the Recipient's possession or control has been returned,' it creates uncertainty about the process of returning Confidential Information upon request. The obligation to return and certify is removed. This now conflicts with the 'Confidential Information remains at all times the property of the Discloser', which requires an eventual return of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Parties to whom the information is imparted must be warned that include d ‘insider information ,’ regarding publicly listed and traded shares may preclud e them from such trade in those shares . The Confidential Informatio n remains at all times the property of the Discloser, and other than as provided in this agreement, no rights in respect of the Confidential Information are granted or conveyed to the Recipient. Confidentiality & Non -Disclosure Agreement ICT Strategic Consulting 2 of 2 Ph: +61 (02) 9591 1464 56 Minnamorra Avenue Earlwood NSW 2206 Australia www.ICTStrategicConsulting.com In the event that the Recipient is legally required to disclo se any Confidential Information, the Recipient must immediately notify the Discloser of that fact.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a process for returning confidential information, ensuring the Discloser regained ownership. The changed text removes the obligation for the Recipient to return the Confidential Information within 7 days of a written request, and provide certification of return. This contradicts the contradicted text stating 'The Confidential Information remains at all times the property of the Discloser'. Without a mechanism for return, the Discloser's property rights are undermined, creating a clear contradiction as defined in the Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties make no warranties or representations to each other in relation to any Confidential Information including (without limitation) as to the accur acy or completeness of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties make no warranties or representations to each other.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'in relation to any Confidential Information including (without limitation) as to the accur acy or completeness of any Confidential Information' broadens the scope of the disclaimer. The agreement becomes ambiguous if the parties exchange documents that are not technically defined as 'Confidential Information'. The accuracy or completeness of these documents may now give rise to implied representations if the disclaimer is too broad. This contradicts the earlier purpose of information sharing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BACKGROUND The parties intend to investigate a Project or Business Opportunity concerning: Potential investment , sale or acquisition of ICT businesses in Australia . For this purpose (“Purpose ”), the parties intend to make confidential information available to each other , and wish to protect this information in accordance with this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the disclaimer of warranties and representations to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text removes this limitation, broadening the scope of the disclaimer to all information exchanged between the parties. The contradicted text states that the parties 'intend to make confidential information available to each other , and wish to protect this information in accordance with this agreement.' By broadening the disclaimer, the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the implied representations concerning the accuracy and completeness of documents that are not defined as 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction and therefore falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
